title: Conroe ISD Board Meeting — June 18, 2024

# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2024-06-18
*Regular Meeting — Unofficial Notes*
*Based on auto-generated transcript. Not an official record.*
*Generated 2026-03-28 17:10 UTC*

---

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — June 18, 2024

**Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions.

---

## Board Members Present (Quorum Confirmed)
- Scott Henry Hubert (President)
- Misty Odenweller
- Stacey Chase
- Theresa Dungan
- Tiffany Nelson
- Datyon Williams
- Skeeter Wagman

---

## 1. Opening Items
- **Invocation:** Led by Trustee Datyon Williams
- **Pledge of Allegiance:** Led by Trustee Odenweller

---

## 2. Special Recognitions — Ambassador Awards

### A. Child Nutrition Department
Presented by Robin Hughes. Honorees:
- **Martine Plunkett** — Associate at Houser Elementary (7 years of service)
- **Gina Baker** — Manager at Glenlock Elementary (3 years; stepped up to manager role)
- **Megan Medina** — Manager at Veterans Memorial (4 years; not present)
- **Kelly Highberger** — Associate at The Woodlands High School (3 years; not present)

### B. Custodial & Maintenance Department
Presented by Marshall Shrader. Honorees:
- **Michael Figueroa** — Athletic Ground Supervisor (14 years)
- **William Dwayne Faulber** — Maintenance (joined 2022)
- **Maria de la Flores** — Custodial (since 2014)
- **Margarita Guajardo** — Custodial (since 2017; also named Humanitarian of the Year)
- **Buddy Herman** — Custodial at Suchma Elementary (since campus opened; nominated for Teacher Achieving Excellence two years running; honored by Habitat for Humanity)
- **Miguel Gonzalez Castillo** — HVAC Lead Technician (since 2016)
- **Robert Washington** — Irrigation Team

---

## 3. Citizen Participation (Public Comments)
Approximately 27–30 speakers signed up. Key themes:

### School Naming
- **Lorraine Rice** advocated for naming the new elementary school after **Anthony Thatch**, a recently retired CISD janitor who served 44 years and immigrated from Cambodia. Noted her Facebook post was shared ~900 times.
- **John Meredith** (President, Conroe Community Cemetery Restoration Project) advocated for naming a school after **Midy J. Campbell**, the first African-American woman principal in Conroe (1918), whose school name was changed to Booker T. Washington in 1927.
- **Victor Yore** supported the Midy J. Campbell nomination.
- **Craig Eissler** advocated for naming the Woodlands feeder elementary after his father, **Rob Eissler**, who served 18 years on the CISD board and 10 years in the Texas Legislature (6 as chairman of the Public Education Committee).
- **Janice Chroman** and **Keith Cartright** advocated for naming a school after **Lynn Cartright**, a 40-year CISD teacher at Conroe High School.

### Gender Identity / Title IX Policy
- **Jason Roacho** (Woodlands Pride board member) opposed the proposed gender fluidity policy, directing encouraging words to LGBTQ+ students.
- **Landon Richie** (Transgender Education Network of Texas) cited extensive case law supporting trans student rights and warned about risk to federal funding.
- **Saia Parekh** (student) opposed the policy, citing harm to trans students and calling out contradictions with the district's "all means all" motto.
- **Lana Lindsay** (Woodlands High School student) challenged the policy's reliance on "biological facts" and cited Trevor Project data on suicide attempt reduction when pronouns are respected.
- **Jared Burton** (Katie ISD student) testified about living under a similar policy at Katy ISD, warning of dangerous consequences and noting Katy's federal investigation.
- **Mandy Giles** (Resource Parents of Trans Youth) estimated ~1,000+ trans/non-binary students in CISD and cited research showing higher sexual assault rates for trans students with bathroom restrictions.
- **Elizabeth Fouse** (therapist, 17+ years) described harmful mental health impacts on LGBTQ+ youth.
- **Martin Atwab** (Citizens Defending Freedom attorney) argued the new Title IX guidance did not go through proper legislative process and should not be followed.
- **Robert Phillips** supported board members standing against gender ideology.

### Book Removals / Level Three Appeal
- **Juliet Lucus** (incoming freshman, College Park High School) read a list of banned books and advocated for restoring them, emphasizing representation and diverse voices.
- **Earl Hensel** supported reinstating books including *Slaughterhouse-Five*, *Brave New World*, *Beloved*, and *The Color Purple*.
- **Deborah Liber** strongly opposed book removals, arguing no parent or trustee should impose personal values on all students.
- **Kelly Loftus** noted CISD has banned more books than any other Texas district, with many challenges originating from a single trustee (~80 challenges).
- **Rachel Walker** (Woodlands Pride) announced the upcoming Woodlands Pride Community Library opening with 300+ titles.
- **Angela Asen** defended *Brave New World*, *The Color Purple*, and *Beloved* from personal experience.
- **Cameron Samuels** (invited by a CISD student) connected book bans to targeting of LGBTQ+ students and people of color.
- **Crystal Lowry** (rising 5th grader reading at 10th grade level) spoke about the importance of representation in books.

### Special Education
- **Amber Fusa** criticized the district's handling of her autistic son's case following a level three hearing, noting the district did not acknowledge Autism Acceptance Month.
- **Mark Fusa** challenged the board's decision not to discipline a school counselor who allegedly violated policy and his son's constitutional rights, questioning why students are held to higher standards than employees.

---

## 4. Consent Agenda
- **Item D** (Communities in Schools contract) was pulled from consent for separate discussion.
- **Remaining consent items: APPROVED unanimously** by voice/hand vote.

### Item 4D — Communities in Schools (CIS) Contract
Dr. Taylor presented the contract for 16 campuses at $26,500/site ($424,000 total to CISD). CIS supplements remaining salary costs.

**Discussion:**
- Trustee Nelson raised concerns about spending $424,000 given the district's ~$17 million projected budget shortfall.
- Dr. Taylor explained services include: backpack buddy program, attendance assistance, home visits, behavior assistance, food pantries, clothing closets, mobile markets, and Social Service referrals.
- Hiring equivalent district staff would cost over $1 million.
- Trustee Williams noted the supplemental value from the CIS partnership.
- Trustee Odenweller suggested exploring additional voluntary organizations to supplement services.
- Clarified: Contract is for the **same 16 campuses** (down from 20 last year), not additional campuses.

**Vote: APPROVED unanimously.**

---

## 5. Naming of New CISD Campuses

### Discussion (Item 5A)
Board members discussed nominations received through surveys, emails, and public comment. Dr. Blakelock provided an overview of the naming process and policies.

Names discussed by trustees:
- **Lynn Cartright** — 40-year Conroe High School teacher
- **Anthony Thatch** — Retired janitor, 44 years of service (received 758 survey votes, highest on the list)
- **Midy J. Campbell** — First African-American woman principal in Conroe (1918); name was removed from a school in 1927 (brought to board's attention by Trustee Wagman, not on original survey)
- **Rob Eissler** — Former CISD board member (18 years), Texas State Representative (10 years, 6 as Public Education Committee chair); instrumental in bringing The Woodlands High School, Woodforest Bank Stadium, and Lone Star College-Montgomery County to the area
- **Timber Mill** — Geographic/historical name reflecting the area's timber industry heritage

Dr. Null noted future naming opportunities exist for schools opening in subsequent years and existing unnamed facilities.

### Item 5B — Name New High School (Conroe Area)
**Motion:** Name the new high school **Timber Mill High School**
- **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.** 🎉

### Item 5C — Name New Elementary (Oak Ridge / Caney Creek Feeders)
- **First Motion:** Name the school **Anthony Thatch Elementary**
  - Vote: 3 in favor, 4 opposed. **Motion FAILED.**
- **Second Motion:** Name the school **Midy J. Campbell Elementary**
  - Brief discussion about whether her married name (Campbell) or maiden name (Turner) was preferred; confirmed Campbell was her married name.
  - **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.** 🎉

### Item 5D — Name New Elementary (The Woodlands Feeder)
- **Motion:** Name the school **Rob Eissler Elementary**
  - Discussion included Trustee Nelson expressing preference for recognizing someone from further back in history but noting there won't be another Woodlands feeder school.
  - Trustee Wagman detailed Eissler's specific contributions to The Woodlands area.
  - President Hubert noted Eissler's name has appeared on the short list nearly every year for 12 years.
  - **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.** 🎉

---

## 6. Capital Improvement Updates (Item 6A)
Presented by Mr. Foster.

| Project | Status |
|---------|--------|
| **Safety & Security (2019 bond, final installment)** | On schedule; ready for August school start |
| **Janet K. Bartlett Elementary** | Opens August 2024; substantially complete, furniture being moved in |
| **Eissler Elementary (Flex School #24)** | Opens August 2025; building pad underway |
| **Campbell Elementary** | Opens August 2025; foundations being laid |
| **Grand Oaks 7-8-9 Grade Campus** | Opens January 2027; building pad under construction |
| **Campus Renovations** (David, Lamar, Houser, Rice Elementaries) | On schedule; AC work, kitchen renovation (Lamar), roofing |
| **Conroe High School Major Renovation** | Fine Arts Wing ready for July summer practices; Dining Commons on track for August; Phase 3 demolition of tower underway; New front entrance expected during 2025-26 school year; Overall completion end of December 2025 |

**Tennis Courts Discussion:**
- Portables occupy current tennis court site at Conroe HS until Timber Mill HS opens (August 2027).
- Plan underway to build a varsity tennis complex at the Conroe HS Ninth Grade Campus using bond funds.
- Competitive sealed bid process planned; board approval expected in August.
- Campus administration, athletics, and tennis parents are all supportive of this plan.

---

## 7. Purchasing Items

### Items 7A-7D (Vendor Pool Expansions)
Presented by Karen Garza. Four RFPs to supplement/expand vendor pools:
- **7A:** Instructional Supplies and Materials for Classrooms
- **7B:** Supplemental Contracted Educational and Professional Development Services
- **7C:** CTE Construction Instructional Supplies and Equipment
- **7D:** Catering Services

Contract terms firm through June 2025 with four additional one-year renewal terms.

**Initial Motion** to approve all four together (7A-7D): **FAILED** (3 in favor).

Items then voted on individually:

**Discussion on 7A** centered on Savvas (formerly Pearson) as a vendor:
- Trustee Nelson raised concerns that Savvas had not yet made promised changes to biology textbook language from the prior month's meeting.
- Dr. Upshaw clarified the biology textbook purchase is separate from this vendor list approval and is pending State Board of Education approval (meeting June 27).
- If SBOE does not approve the biology book, the district will bring an alternative to the board.
- Savvas provides materials for math K-8, social studies K-5, English 6-12, science K-12, and state assessment rescoring; removing them as a vendor would create significant gaps.

| Item | Vote |
|------|------|
| **7A** | **APPROVED unanimously** |
| **7B** | **APPROVED unanimously** |
| **7C** | **APPROVED unanimously** |
| **7D** | **APPROVED unanimously** |

### Item 7E — Website Design and Hosting Services (Apptegy)
Presented by Dr. Blakelock. Current WordPress website facing security vulnerabilities as plugins cease updates for WordPress Classic.

- Initial expenditure: ~$200,000 for design, migration, and first year
- Annual ongoing costs significantly lower than current ~$61,000/year system
- Five-year total: ~$478,000 (compared to $411,000 for first two years of current WordPress site)
- Includes mobile app and social media management integration
- New district website launch planned for January 2025; campus rollout on rolling basis
- **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.**

### Item 7F — Apptegy/Thrillshare & ThoughtExchange (Fulcrum Management Solutions)
Dr. Blakelock presented request to add Fulcrum Management Solutions (parent company of ThoughtExchange) to the over-$50K vendor list.

- Engagement platform for community feedback using AI-powered analysis of open-ended responses
- Six-month pilot: ~$42,000; 32 exchanges conducted with ~2,500 participants
- Requesting another six-month contract to evaluate fit
- Maximum calendar year expenditure: $127,128 (includes contingency for possible year-long contract)
- Potential uses: attendance boundary process, website redesign feedback, employee onboarding/exit surveys

**Vote: APPROVED unanimously.**

---

## 8. Financial Report (Item 8A)
Presented by Karen Garza for month ending May 31, 2024.

| Fund | Total Assets |
|------|-------------|
| General Fund | $333.7 million |
| Debt Service | $78.4 million |
| Child Nutrition | $16.1 million |
| Self-Funded Insurance | $13.7 million |

- **2023 Bond Referendum:** $228.95 million encumbered/expended; ~$1.7 billion remaining
- **Self-Funded Insurance:** Good month in May; revenues exceeded expenditures by $822,000; surplus of $2.3 million heading into summer
- **Investments:** Par value just over $1 billion; pools yielding 5.46%; combined portfolio at 5.37%; 90-day T-bill at 5.23%

---

## 9. Board Policy and Instructional Items

### Item 9A — Adopt Revised Board Policy EIA (Academic Achievement, Retention, and Promotion)
Expands standards-based report cards from grades K-1 to **K-2** (previously grades 2 was grouped with 2-4).

**Discussion:**
- Dr. Winkler explained benefits: better identification of skill mastery, earlier interventions, teachers better able to speak to specific student strengths/weaknesses in MTSS meetings
- Standards-based reporting has been fully implemented in first grade since 2021-22
- 19 campus principals surveyed; all ultimately supported expansion to second grade
- Trustee Hubert shared positive personal experience as a CISD parent with standards-based reporting
- Report card format change does not affect how teaching/grouping is done—only how progress is reported to parents
- No plans to extend beyond second grade unless board requests

**Vote: APPROVED 6-1** (Trustee Odenweller opposed).

### Item 9B — First Reading: Local Policy Manual Update 123
Information item only (first reading). Eight local board policies being updated, primarily implementing regulations from the last legislative session. Policies include:
- BBD (Board Members Training), BBFA (Ethics/Conflicts), CKC (Safety/Emergency Plans), CKA (Security Personnel), CQC (Technology Resources), DEC (Employment Practices), DEC (Compensation/Leaves), EH (Homebound Instruction)

Will return in July for adoption. **No vote required.**

---

## 9C. Level Three Consolidated Appeal Hearing — Book Removals

### Background
Consolidated hearing for complaints filed by **Erin Miller**, **Andrea Young**, **DD Fox**, and **Teresa Neeman** (proxy: Deborah Liber, as Neeman was in Cincinnati scoring AP exams).

Complainants disagreed with the informal Instructional Materials Review Committee's decision to remove 19 novels from use as instructional materials. They requested:
1. Books be re-evaluated through a formal reconsideration process
2. Books be reinstated as instructional materials

**Level Two ruling** (hearing officer Greg Kouchan): Committee complied with board policy EFA; formal reconsideration process is for removal requests, not reinstatement; denied relief.

All board members affirmed they could be impartial.

### The 19 Books at Issue
*Slaughterhouse-Five*, *My Sister's Keeper*, *The Bluest Eye*, *Perks of Being a Wallflower*, *The Color Purple*, *Beloved*, *Homegoing*, *Brave New World*, *Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close*, *Girl in Translation*, *I Am Not Your Perfect Mexican Daughter*, *Salvage the Bones*, *Sold*, *The Hate U Give*, *The Kite Runner*, *The Underground Railroad*, *We Are the Ants*, *Where the Crawdads Sing*, *Speak*

### Complainant Presentations (10 minutes each)

**Andrea Young** — Defended *Slaughterhouse-Five*:
- Classic in the American literary canon; on numerous "best of" lists
- Based on author's WWII experiences; addresses war, PTSD, free will
- Previously ruled not obscene (1979 district court); Supreme Court ordered return to libraries in *Island Trees v. Pico* (1982)
- Argued the informal review process facilitates censorship; committee members didn't all read the books

**DD Fox** — Defended *Speak* and *My Sister's Keeper*:
- Read the passage from *Speak* used to justify removal; argued it depicts sexual assault (not sex) told from a 13-year-old's perspective
- *My Sister's Keeper* incorrectly classified as "adult" based on Titlewave software
- Criticized committee composition (few secondary-level educators, no meeting recordings)
- Warned of negative national attention and potential First Amendment litigation

**Deborah Liber** (proxy for Teresa Neeman) — Defended multiple titles:
- Committee members did not read books; relied on biased sources (Booklooks.org/Moms for Liberty blog, Common Sense Media)
- Committee applied criteria inconsistently and did not evaluate books as a whole per the Miller test
- 79% removal rate in informal review vs. 21% in formal reconsideration
- Cited Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals ruling (Llano County) supporting return of books to shelves
- Drew parallels between Llano County case and CISD's situation regarding board member influence

**Erin Miller** — Defended *The Bluest Eye*, *Perks of Being a Wallflower*, *We Are the Ants*, *Where the Crawdads Sing*, and others:
- Questioned whether removals were politically motivated by outside groups
- Noted AP course audit requirements could be jeopardized
- Requested books be returned or at minimum restored to formal challenge process
- Asked that future review committees include parent volunteers (not just CISD employees) and that members read books in their entirety

### Administration Presentation
**Dr. Jared Lambert** (Director of Information Systems):
- Appeals filed under EFA (instructional materials), not EFB (library materials)
- Informal review committees are standard practice for ensuring consistency
- Committee made "best decisions they could" following board policy
- Level two hearing officer confirmed committee complied with EFA local
- Encouraged board to "respect and validate their work"

### Board Discussion (Significant and Heated)

**Trustee Williams** raised concerns about racial profiling in book selections:
- Of 19 books challenged, 9 (47%) were by minority authors or contained LGBTQ+ content
- Argued this was not representative of the ~5,000-book collection and suggested systemic targeting
- Drew analogy to racial profiling in policing
- Stated: "I'm calling the spade a spade... the policy lends itself to discrimination"
- Noted Mark Twain's works (which use racial slurs) were not removed

**Trustee Chase** questioned how *The Color Purple* (Pulitzer Prize, National Book Award, on AP exam 11 times) could be deemed educationally unsuitable. Dr. Lambert cited "explicit language" and "vivid depiction of a rape scene" as committee's basis.

**Trustee Dungan** asked about committee timeline and procedures; expressed concern about the informal process.

**Trustee Nelson** noted some removed books (including *Brave New World*, *Beloved*, *The Hate U Give*) are still available in CISD libraries, just removed from instructional use.

**Trustee Wagman** noted she couldn't find where the policy specifically excludes the challenged books and acknowledged the policy needs "complete overhaul."

**President Hubert** stated he hadn't read the books and felt he should support the committee's implementation of the policy, but acknowledged "glaring issues" needing future attention. He proposed revisiting policy at a later date.

**Key exchange:** When Trustee Williams stated the policy "lends itself to discrimination," Trustee Odenweller took exception, saying Williams was "calling the committee racist." Williams clarified he was criticizing the policy outcomes, not individuals.

### Votes on Level Three Appeals

Each complainant's appeal was voted on separately. For each, a motion to reinstate books as instructional materials was made and failed, followed by a motion to uphold the committee's decision that passed.

| Complainant | Books | Motion to Reinstate | Motion to Uphold Committee |
|------------|-------|-------------------|--------------------------|
| **DD Fox** | *My Sister's Keeper*, *Speak* | **FAILED** (3-4) | **PASSED** (4-3) |
| **Erin Miller** | *The Bluest Eye*, *Perks of Being a Wallflower*, *We Are the Ants*, *Where the Crawdads Sing* | **FAILED** (3-4) | **PASSED** (4-3) |
| **Andrea Young** | *Slaughterhouse-Five* | **FAILED** (3-4) | **PASSED** (4-3) |
| **Teresa Neeman** | *Underground Railroad*, *The Hate U Give*, *The Color Purple*, *Sold*, *Salvage the Bones*, *I Am Not Your Perfect Mexican Daughter*, *The Kite Runner*, *Homegoing*, *Girl in Translation*, *Extremely Loud and Incredibly Close*, *Brave New World*, *Beloved* | **FAILED** (3-4) | **PASSED** (4-3) |

**Outcome: All 19 books remain removed as instructional materials.** The board will send written notice to complainants.

⚠️ **Notable:** Multiple trustees acknowledged flaws in the policy and procedure, including that committee members did not read the books. A future agenda item to re-evaluate EFA and EFB policies was requested (see Item 13).

---

## Executive Session
Board entered closed session at 11:03 PM under Texas Government Code sections 551.071 and 551.074.

Returned to open session at 11:23 PM.

---

## 12. Post-Executive Session Action Items

### Item 12A — Superintendent Evaluation and Contract
**Motion:** Having completed the superintendent's evaluation, approve amendments to the superintendent's contract as discussed in executive session, including extending the contract for an additional year.
- **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.**

### Item 12B — Purchase of ~20-Acre Parcel (Caney Creek Feeder Zone)
Delegated superintendent to authorize final negotiation and execute contract documents.
- **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.**

### Item 12C — Easement and Right-of-Way Agreement with Blackfin Pipeline LLC
Payment to district: $250,941.
- **Vote: APPROVED unanimously.**

### Item 12D — Resolution Against Title IX Changes (Trustee Nelson)
Trustee Nelson presented a resolution opposing the Biden administration's changes to Title IX regulations.

**Extended and contentious discussion:**
- Trustee Williams submitted a counter-resolution criticizing "politically based agendas at the expense of students" and calling out hypocrisy in board actions on books, vouchers, and other issues.
- Trustee Chase argued the Title IX changes have nothing to do with restrooms and characterized the resolution as "fear mongering."
- Trustee Williams stated Title IX regulations do not mention restrooms and called the resolution political theater.
- Trustee Odenweller argued for protecting students in bathrooms and locker rooms and cited lawsuits against teachers over pronoun usage.
- Miss Gadus (legal counsel) noted a current injunction is in place against enforcement of the new Title IX guidance documents in Texas; anticipated regulations would also be enjoined.
- Trustee Nelson read from a same-day update from the Texas Attorney General's office regarding a ruling covering all Texas school districts.
- President Hubert raised fiscal concerns about potential litigation costs if the district takes a stance against federal law.

**Procedural votes:**
1. **Motion to reject all resolutions on the table:** Made and seconded.
2. **Amendment:** Wait for clear legal direction from attorney general and resolution of pending lawsuits before taking action.
   - **Amendment vote: PASSED 4-1-2** (2 abstentions)
3. **Amended motion (reject all resolutions and wait for legal clarity): PASSED** (exact count unclear from transcript; appeared to carry).

**Outcome: No Title IX resolution adopted. Board will wait for final legal direction.**

### Item 12E — Gender Fluidity and Parental Notification Policy (Trustee Odenweller)
Trustee Odenweller requested discussion and development of a local policy addressing gender fluidity and parental notification.

**Discussion:**
- Dr. Null stated he has never received a complaint from a parent that a campus withheld information about their child's gender identity; campuses handle these situations individually, privately, and with families.
- Trustee Chase challenged whether there is an actual problem occurring in CISD schools requiring this policy.
- Trustee Williams stated: "Policies isolate or target one specific demographic."
- Trustee Odenweller argued written guidance is needed for consistency across campuses and teacher protection.
- Exchange became heated, with personal remarks exchanged between trustees.

**Outcome: No motion was made. No policy development was initiated.** ⚠️

---

## 13. Future Agenda Item Requests
- **Trustee Williams** requested a future agenda item to **re-evaluate board policies EFA and EFB** (instructional materials and library materials policies) in light of issues identified during the Level Three book appeal hearing.

---

## Meeting Adjourned
**Time: 12:07 AM (June 19, 2024)**
